name: Worldline European Payment Technology REST API
what_it_does: Worldline European payment technology REST API for banks, merchants, governments, and financial institutions across Europe, Asia, and Latin America to process card payments, manage payment terminals, enable open banking connectivity, and access financial services — enabling automated payment processing, terminal management, account-to-account payments, and merchant services through Worldline's pan-European payment technology platform serving 1M+ merchants and 1,200+ financial institution clients. Enables AI agents to manage payment processing for card acceptance and authorization automation, handle terminal management for POS device fleet automation, access open banking for PSD2 account access and payment initiation automation, retrieve financial services for banking-as-a-service automation, manage fraud detection for transaction risk scoring automation, handle acquiring for merchant card acceptance automation, access digital banking for white-label bank service automation, retrieve reporting for payment analytics and reconciliation automation, manage loyalty for payment-linked rewards program automation, and integrate Worldline with core banking, e-commerce, and government payment platforms for enterprise payment infrastructure automation.
use_cases: Processing card and account-to-account payments for European enterprise merchant automation agents
Managing payment terminal fleet for retail and hospitality POS automation agents
Accessing PSD2 open banking APIs for European financial data aggregation automation agents
Integrating white-label digital banking services for bank digital transformation automation agents
not_for: Small merchants without Worldline acquiring agreement (Worldline focuses on mid-market to enterprise)
Pure crypto payments (Worldline is traditional card and bank payment infrastructure)
Consumer-direct banking apps (Worldline provides B2B payment infrastructure to banks, not consumers)
best_when: A European bank, enterprise merchant, or government entity wanting AI agents to process card payments, manage payment terminals, access open banking, and integrate digital banking services through Worldline's pan-European payment technology platform.
avoid_when: REGIONAL PRODUCT FRAGMENTATION: Worldline has different product lines from Ingenico acquisition and legacy Atos; automated uniform European API assumption creates product_line_mismatch for different European markets; automated must identify correct Worldline product for specific country and use case. ACQUIRER AGREEMENT REQUIRED: Worldline card acquiring requires merchant acquiring agreement; automated self-service merchant acquiring assumption creates requires_acquiring_agreement; automated must establish Worldline acquiring relationship. TERMINAL MANAGEMENT REQUIRES HARDWARE: Worldline terminal management APIs assume physical Worldline/Ingenico POS hardware; automated software-only terminal assumption creates no_device_found; automated must have Worldline-compatible terminal hardware. PSD2 CONSENT IS MANDATORY: Open banking data access requires customer PSD2 consent; automated non-consent data access assumption creates consent_required; automated must implement PSD2-compliant consent flow.

auth.notes: Worldline uses API key + OAuth2 for payment REST API. REST API with JSON. Bezons, France HQ. Founded 1972 as Atos spin-off. NYSE Euronext: WLN. Products: Payment acquiring, terminal solutions, digital banking, open banking, fraud management, financial services. SDKs: Java, PHP, Python, JS. 1M+ merchants. 1,200+ financial institution clients. Acquired Ingenico (2020), Six Payment (2022). Competes with Adyen and Nexi for European payment processing.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer sandbox for approved partners; production requires contract
pricing.paid_tiers: MDR-based acquiring fees; terminal rental fees; annual platform fees; custom enterprise pricing
pricing.notes: Bezons FR. Euronext:WLN. MDR + platform fees. 1M+ merchants. European payment leader.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; authorization and terminal management APIs throttled per tier

known_agent_gotchas: PRODUCT LINE FRAGMENTATION: Worldline has multiple distinct API platforms from acquisitions (Worldline Online Payments, Ingenico Connect, Six Payment); automated single-API assumption creates wrong endpoint for specific product line; automated must identify correct API product for use case and country

TERMINAL MESSAGE PROTOCOL VARIES: Payment terminal communication uses different protocols (NEXO, SPDH, proprietary); automated REST-only terminal assumption creates protocol_mismatch for specific terminal models; automated must verify terminal protocol for specific Worldline/Ingenico hardware

MANDATE MANAGEMENT IS COMPLEX: European direct debit mandates (SEPA) require mandate management with debtor consent; automated simple SEPA debit assumption creates mandate_required for first-time debits; automated must implement SEPA mandate creation and management

COUNTRY-SPECIFIC LOCAL PAYMENT METHODS: Different European countries have local payment methods (iDEAL NL, Bancontact BE, Giropay DE) with separate integration requirements; automated pan-European uniform checkout assumption creates payment_method_unavailable for country-specific methods; automated must implement country-specific payment method flows

3DS2 IS MANDATORY FOR E-COMMERCE: Worldline requires 3DS2 authentication for card-not-present e-commerce; automated non-3DS assumption creates authentication_required in European markets; automated must implement 3DS2 challenge flow for compliant European card payments
agent_readiness.security_notes: European payment technology. PSD2, PCI-DSS, GDPR. API key + OAuth2. EU. Card, bank, and merchant payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: European payments. PSD2, PCI-DSS, GDPR. API key + OAuth2. EU. Card and bank payment data.
